title: task
description:
ip_coap.py:189-206 - In _next_observation_or_close(), when the close event wins the race, the cancelled next_task is not awaited before returning None. Per asyncio best practices, cancelled tasks should be awaited to allow cleanup.

Failure scenario: When a subscription is closed while waiting for the next notification, the next_task is cancelled but not awaited. This can cause 'Task was destroyed but it is pending!' warnings and potential resource leaks if the underlying iterator has cleanup code.

Fix: Add 'with suppress(asyncio.CancelledError): await next_task' after cancelling when close_task wins.
